name: Dome of the Rock
description: The Dome of the Rock is a significant Islamic shrine located on the Temple Mount in the Old City of Jerusalem. Completed in 691–692 CE, it is one of the oldest examples of Islamic architecture. The Dome of the Rock is built over the Foundation Stone, which holds immense religious significance in both Judaism and Islam. In Islamic tradition, it is believed to be the spot from which the Prophet Muhammad ascended to heaven during his Night Journey (Isra and Mi'raj). The structure itself is not a mosque, but a shrine housing the sacred rock. Its golden dome and intricate mosaics make it a visually stunning landmark and a powerful symbol of Islam in Jerusalem. The Foundation Stone is also believed to be the place where Abraham prepared to sacrifice his son Isaac (or Ishmael, according to Islamic belief). The site represents a convergence of Abrahamic faiths and their shared history, making it a focal point of religious and political significance. The Dome of the Rock's architectural design and artistic ornamentation reflect the early development of Islamic art and its influence on subsequent architectural styles. The surrounding Temple Mount, known to Muslims as Haram al-Sharif (the Noble Sanctuary), is considered the third holiest site in Islam.
subtitle: Ascension Point and Sacred Foundation Stone

keyMyths.title: The Night Journey (Isra and Mi'raj)
keyMyths.description: The Prophet Muhammad journeyed from Mecca to Jerusalem in a single night, then ascended to heaven from the Foundation Stone. During this journey, he met with prophets and received divine commandments.

keyMyths.title: Abraham's Sacrifice
keyMyths.description: Muslims believe that Abraham prepared to sacrifice his son Ishmael (rather than Isaac) on the Foundation Stone as an act of obedience to God. This event is commemorated during Eid al-Adha.

sources.author: Al-Tabari
sources.work: History of the Prophets and Kings
